findings.evidence: `Subcoalgebra.iSup_toSubmodule` is defined at `TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Subcoalgebra/Lattice.lean:158`, and Mathlib has `Submodule.map_iSup` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Module/Submodule/Map.lean:229`; the PR only adds `map_sup`/`map_finset_sup` at `Map.lean:142` and `Map.lean:150`.
findings.fix: Add the general lemma `@[simp] theorem map_iSup {ι : Sort*} (f : C →ₗc[R] D) (A : ι → Subcoalgebra R C) : (⨆ i, A i).map f = ⨆ i, (A i).map f` and derive `map_finset_sup` from it via `Finset.sup_eq_iSup` if the finite spelling is still needed.
findings.issue: `map_finset_sup` proves only the finite-specialized join statement, while `Subcoalgebra` already has arbitrary suprema and the underlying proof is just `Submodule.map_iSup`.

summary: The image API stops at finite joins even though the surrounding subcoalgebra lattice and Mathlib submodule API support arbitrary suprema, so the finite lemma is at the wrong generality level.
